description:
An open-source alternative to Claude Cowork, powered by OpenCode

OpenWork is an extensible, open-source “Claude Work” style system for knowledge workers.
It’s a native desktop app that runs OpenCode under the hood, but presents it as a clean, guided workflow:
- pick a workspace
- start a run
- watch progress + plan updates
- approve permissions when needed
- reuse what works (templates + skills)
The goal: make “agentic work” feel like a product, not a terminal.
Highlights
- Keeps workspace shell navigation reachable across dashboard and session flows, with improved sidebar layout behavior in the app.
- Polishes the Den web experience with refined pricing and capability cards, plus split docs entrypoints for technical and non-technical audiences.
Fixes & polish
- Fully clears desktop reset state on relaunch to make recovery flows more reliable.
- Brands the macOS dev app distinctly so local development builds are easier to identify.
- Seeds Control Chrome as chrome-devtools to make MCP browser tooling work more predictably.
Packaging / ops
- Ships patch release v0.11.144 with refreshed package versions and lockfile updates.
- Re-ran the release workflow for the existing tag without bumping versions again after transient npm/cache service issues.
Compare:
https://github.com/different-ai/openwork/compare/v0.11.143...v0.11.144
